    Home › Lines › Princess Cruises

    PCL Princess Cruises

    United States · Carnival Corporation · HQ: Fort Lauderdale / Los Angeles

    The Love Boat line — destination cruising at scale, crowned by the Italian-built Sphere class.

    Ships here13
    Biggest shipSun Princess
    Group / allianceCarnival Corporation
    Founded1965
    MarkPCL
    Side by side

    The fleet at true scale

    Every giant Princess Cruises operates on this site, drawn to the same scale — longest hull on the left. Tap any silhouette.

    Sun Princess
    Star Princess
    Discovery Princess

    Ruby Princess
    Caribbean Princess
    Grand Princess
    The whole fleet

    Every Princess Cruises ship, in brief

    Princess Cruises sails 17 ships as of mid-2026. The Sphere class crowns the fleet — Sun Princess (2024) and flagship Star Princess (2025), with a third on order — above six Royal-class ships (Royal, Regal, Majestic, Sky, Enchanted, Discovery Princess), the Grand and Crown classes that defined 2000s Princess, and the smaller Coral-class pair for the Panama Canal.

    We profile the flagships in full below — the rest of the fleet joins ships.fyi class by class.

    The fleet

    What Princess Cruises operates

    Grid
    1 in fleet

    Sun Princess

    Fincantieri · In service

    Mediterranean and Caribbean seasons; christened by Hannah Waddingham in April 2024.

    Full specification →
    1 in fleet

    Star Princess

    Fincantieri · In service

    Mediterranean inaugural season, Caribbean winters from Fort Lauderdale, Alaska from 2026 — she made her first Panama Canal transit in April 2026.

    Full specification →
    1 in fleet

    Discovery Princess

    Fincantieri · In service

    Alaska summers from Seattle and Mexican Riviera winters from Los Angeles.

    Full specification →
    1 in fleet

    Enchanted Princess

    Fincantieri · In service

    Caribbean itineraries from Fort Lauderdale; Europe in summer.

    Full specification →
    1 in fleet

    Sky Princess

    Fincantieri · In service

    Caribbean winters from Fort Lauderdale, European summers.

    Full specification →
    1 in fleet

    Majestic Princess

    Fincantieri · In service

    Australia and New Zealand seasons from Sydney; Alaska in the northern summer.

    Full specification →
    1 in fleet

    Regal Princess

    Fincantieri · In service

    Caribbean and Northern Europe itineraries.

    Full specification →
    1 in fleet

    Royal Princess

    Fincantieri · In service

    Caribbean and Mediterranean itineraries; christened by the Duchess of Cambridge in Southampton, June 2013.

    Full specification →
    1 in fleet

    Diamond Princess

    Mitsubishi Heavy Industries · In service

    Japan and Asia in the northern summer, Australia in the southern summer.

    Full specification →
    1 in fleet

    Sapphire Princess

    Mitsubishi Heavy Industries · In service

    Asia, Alaska and Australia deployments.

    Full specification →
    1 in fleet

    Ruby Princess

    Fincantieri · In service

    Caribbean, Alaska and Panama Canal itineraries.

    Full specification →
    1 in fleet

    Caribbean Princess

    Fincantieri · In service

    Caribbean itineraries year-round, as the name implies.

    Full specification →
    1 in fleet

    Grand Princess

    Fincantieri · In service

    Alaska summers and California-based itineraries.

    Full specification →
    Good to know

    Princess Cruises, in a few facts

    • The 1970s TV series The Love Boat was filmed aboard Princess ships and made the brand a household name before most people had ever cruised.
    • Its Sphere-class flagships Sun Princess and Star Princess are the largest cruise ships ever built in Italy.
    • A pioneer of large-scale Alaska cruising, pairing ships with its own network of wilderness lodges.
    Sources

    Primary

    • Princess Cruises — ships ↗

    Further reading

    • Princess Cruises — Wikipedia ↗

Port safety Ships in storm The Silhouette Quiz Methodology Matchups Icon vs Titanic Legend vs Icon Titanic vs Queen Mary 2 Seawise Giant vs Icon Ever Given vs Ever Alot Emma Mærsk vs MSC Irina Blog The six days that stopped world trade Why the biggest ships stopped growing
    About the data. Every specification is compiled from operator, builder and registry sources and cited per row. Fleet and status figures are snapshots, marked ≈ where approximate and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. Ranks never mix units — cruise ships rank by GT, container ships by TEU, tankers by DWT. All silhouettes are original works of ships.fyi, drawn to true scale. Guides may contain clearly marked affiliate links; they never change what we recommend. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
    © 2026 ships.fyi
    RecordsExplained
